summary: No blocking findings. The slice ships a discoverable `pr-runtime` skill, a narrow helper-backed runtime record surface, focused tests, and the doc updates needed to make the workflow real instead of chat-only.

rubric_sections.name: code-quality
findings: The helper stays narrow and local-first: runtime-record persistence, optional worktree creation, port reservation, and QA target lookup.
The workflow logic lives in the new skill package instead of being smeared into root policy or hook logic.
rubric_sections.next_action: Keep future follow-ups scoped to compose orchestration or broader dispatch separately rather than widening this helper opportunistically.

rubric_sections.name: evidence-quality
findings: The ticket links explicit verification commands, the helper tests pass, and a helper smoke path proves QA target publication and port release.
The review and implementation artifacts are ticket-scoped and easy to inspect.
rubric_sections.next_action: Use a future runtime-manager ticket if heavier compose or GitHub-driven PR resolution proof is needed.

rubric_sections.name: integration-readiness
findings: README, runtime docs, inventory, memory, and the shipped global contract now all point at the same workflow and state placement.
The implementation respects existing runtime boundaries by keeping `.harness/state/tickets/*.runtime.json` runtime-only.
rubric_sections.next_action: Archive the ticket after any desired follow-up tickets for compose or GitHub PR auto-resolution are created.

next_action: Mark the ticket done and keep any broader runtime-manager expansion in follow-up tickets.
